**Modane** (French pronunciation: [[mɔ.dan]](/wiki/Help:IPA/French "Help:IPA/French"); [Savoyard](/wiki/Savoyard_dialect "Savoyard dialect"): *Modâna*[[3]](#cite_note-3); [Italian](/wiki/Italian_language "Italian language"): *Modana*) is a [commune](/wiki/Communes_of_France "Communes of France") in the [Savoie](/wiki/Savoie "Savoie") [department](/wiki/Departments_of_France "Departments of France") in the [Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes](/wiki/Auvergne-Rh%C3%B4ne-Alpes "Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes") [region](/wiki/Regions_of_France "Regions of France") in southeastern [France](/wiki/France "France").

The commune is in the [Maurienne](/wiki/Maurienne "Maurienne") Valley, and it also belongs to the [Vanoise National Park](/wiki/Vanoise_National_Park "Vanoise National Park"). It was part of the [Kingdom of Sardinia](/wiki/Kingdom_of_Sardinia "Kingdom of Sardinia") until the [Treaty of Turin](/wiki/Treaty_of_Turin_(1860) "Treaty of Turin (1860)") in 1860.

## Geography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Modane&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Geography")]

### Location

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Modane&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Location")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/a/ae/Modane%2C_panorama_%282015%29.JPG/250px-Modane%2C_panorama_%282015%29.JPG)](/wiki/File:Modane,_panorama_(2015).JPG)

View of Modane from [La Norma](/wiki/La_Norma "La Norma").

The commune of Modane is located in the [Alps](/wiki/Alps "Alps") in the department of [Savoie](/wiki/Savoie "Savoie") between the [Vanoise massif](/wiki/Vanoise_massif "Vanoise massif") to the north and between the [Massif du Mont-Cenis](/w/index.php?title=Massif_du_Mont-Cenis&action=edit&redlink=1 "Massif du Mont-Cenis (page does not exist)") [[fr](https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/Massif_du_Mont-Cenis "fr:Massif du Mont-Cenis")] and the [Massif des Cerces](/wiki/Massif_des_Cerces "Massif des Cerces") to the south. Crossed by the [Arc](/wiki/Arc_(Savoie) "Arc (Savoie)") river, it extends to the doors of the Haute-Maurienne. The issue of the attachment or not of Modane in the [Maurienne](/wiki/Maurienne "Maurienne") Valley or Haute-Maurienne differs depending on the disciplines of economics, geography or geology. For economists, Modane is attached to Haute Maurienne, arguing that the city has a very strong influence on the villages of Haute Maurienne, through economic and administrative infrastructure such as shopping centres, schools or the railway station for example. However, for the great majority of analysts, Modane is a commune attached to the middle part of this valley, both by the relief (encompassing the whole canton is a dug coal-bearing furrow which extends to Saint-Michel-de-Maurienne), and by the industrial history of this sector.[[4]](#cite_note-4)

With an area of 7,104 acres (2,875 ha) [28.74 square kilometres (11.10 sq mi)], the commune extends along a north–south axis on both sides of the valley. To the north, Modane is bounded by the [Roc des Saints Pères](/wiki/Roc_des_Saints_P%C3%A8res "Roc des Saints Pères"), and the [Aiguille de Péclet](/wiki/Aiguille_de_P%C3%A9clet "Aiguille de Péclet") (northwest) and the [Dôme de Polset](/wiki/D%C3%B4me_de_Polset "Dôme de Polset") (northeast). To the south, also from west to east, these are the [Refuge du Mont Thabor](/wiki/Refuge_du_Mont_Thabor "Refuge du Mont Thabor") (marking the boundary with the department of [Hautes-Alpes](/wiki/Hautes-Alpes "Hautes-Alpes")), the Cime de la Planette, the [Pointe du Fréjus](/w/index.php?title=Pointe_du_Fr%C3%A9jus&action=edit&redlink=1 "Pointe du Fréjus (page does not exist)") [[fr](https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pointe_du_Fr%C3%A9jus "fr:Pointe du Fréjus")] and the [Cime du Grand Vallon](/wiki/Punta_Bagn%C3%A0 "Punta Bagnà") (marking the boundary with Italy) which delimit the neighbouring communes of Modane and Italy.

The Modane territory extends in a particular form. Indeed, a relatively short east–west width on the north side of the valley (a few hundred meters to a maximum of 5 kilometres (3.1 mi) between the peaks), is much larger on the south side (about 2 to 8 kilometres (1.2 to 5.0 mi) from the South shore of the CRA to peaks). From north to south, the municipality extends however over a length of around 15 kilometres (9.3 mi).

In Vanoise, the town is dominated by the [Dent Parrachée](/wiki/Dent_Parrach%C3%A9e "Dent Parrachée") at 3,697 metres (12,129 ft), the [Pointe de l'Échelle](/wiki/Pointe_de_l%27%C3%89chelle "Pointe de l'Échelle") at 3,427 metres (11,243 ft), the [Rateau d'Aussois](/w/index.php?title=Rateau_d%27Aussois&action=edit&redlink=1 "Rateau d'Aussois (page does not exist)") [[fr](https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rateau_d%27Aussois "fr:Rateau d'Aussois")] at 3,117 metres (10,226 ft), the Aiguille Doran at 3,039 metres (9,970 ft), the [Dôme de Polset](/wiki/D%C3%B4me_de_Polset "Dôme de Polset") at 3,566 metres (11,699 ft) or the [Pointe Rénod](/wiki/Pointe_R%C3%A9nod "Pointe Rénod") at 3,374 metres (11,070 ft). To the south stand the Pointe de Longecôte at 3,100 metres (10,200 ft), the [Aiguille de Scolette](/wiki/Aiguille_de_Scolette "Aiguille de Scolette") at 3,508 metres (11,509 ft), the Belle Plinier at 3,076 metres (10,092 ft), the Pointe d’Arrondaz at 2,937 metres (9,636 ft) and also the [Pointe du Fréjus](/w/index.php?title=Pointe_du_Fr%C3%A9jus&action=edit&redlink=1 "Pointe du Fréjus (page does not exist)") [[fr](https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pointe_du_Fr%C3%A9jus "fr:Pointe du Fréjus")] at 2,934 metres (9,626 ft).

These peaks, however, leave easy passages, particularly frequented during summer by hiking tourists, either to the [Tarentaise Valley](/wiki/Tarentaise_Valley "Tarentaise Valley") or to [Italy](/wiki/Italy "Italy"). In addition, some welcome [ski resorts](/wiki/Ski_resort "Ski resort") located within walking distance of the town, among which are [Aussois](/wiki/Aussois "Aussois"), [La Norma](/wiki/La_Norma "La Norma") and [Valfréjus](/wiki/Valfr%C3%A9jus "Valfréjus").

### Climate

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Modane&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Climate")]

According to the [Köppen climate classification](/wiki/K%C3%B6ppen_climate_classification "Köppen climate classification"), Modane features a [continental](/wiki/Continental_climate "Continental climate") [mountain climate](/wiki/Mountain_climate "Mountain climate") (Köppen: Dfb), with long, snowy and very cold winters and warm, somewhat dry summers.

### Neighbouring communes

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Modane&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Neighbouring communes")]

The commune of Modane is bordered by nine other communes in France, besides its southern limit marked by the Franco-[Italian](/wiki/Italy "Italy") border. To the west, Modane mainly borders [Saint-André](/wiki/Saint-Andr%C3%A9,_Savoie "Saint-André, Savoie") and [Fourneaux](/wiki/Fourneaux,_Savoie "Fourneaux, Savoie"), but also to the south-west, [Freney](/wiki/Freney "Freney"), [Orelle](/wiki/Orelle "Orelle"), and [Névache](/wiki/N%C3%A9vache "Névache") in the neighbouring department of [Hautes-Alpes](/wiki/Hautes-Alpes "Hautes-Alpes"). To the north, in the [Vanoise National Park](/wiki/Vanoise_National_Park "Vanoise National Park"), the bordering communes are [Saint-Martin-de-Belleville](/wiki/Saint-Martin-de-Belleville "Saint-Martin-de-Belleville") to the north-west, with [Les Allues](/wiki/Les_Allues "Les Allues") and [Pralognan-la-Vanoise](/wiki/Pralognan-la-Vanoise "Pralognan-la-Vanoise") to the north-east. To the east lies [Villarodin-Bourget](/wiki/Villarodin-Bourget "Villarodin-Bourget"), with [Avrieux](/wiki/Avrieux "Avrieux") to the south-east.

#### Roads

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Modane&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Roads")]

The commune is at the French end of the [Fréjus Road Tunnel](/wiki/Fr%C3%A9jus_Road_Tunnel "Fréjus Road Tunnel"), one of the major transalpine [road](/wiki/Road "Road") crossings along with the [Mont Blanc Tunnel](/wiki/Mont_Blanc_Tunnel "Mont Blanc Tunnel"), linking France and [Italy](/wiki/Italy "Italy"). Out of the tunnel, the [A43 autoroute](/wiki/A43_autoroute "A43 autoroute") begins and which leads to [Chambéry](/wiki/Chamb%C3%A9ry "Chambéry") and [Lyon](/wiki/Lyon "Lyon"), also passes on the heights of the commune (in *Charmaix*).

In addition, the [D 1006](/wiki/Route_nationale_6 "Route nationale 6") (former Route Nationale 6), an important route coming from Chambéry, also passes through Modane before continuing up to the [Col du Mont-Cenis](/wiki/Mont_Cenis "Mont Cenis") and the Italian border. The road is also the main axis of the commune, which it crosses through the full length of the centre. However, it has several street names including, from west to east: *Avenue de la Liberté*, *Rue de la République*, *Avenue Jean Jaurès* and the *Cours Aristide Briand*.

#### Railway

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Modane&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Railway")]

The commune of Modane is home to an international railway station, the [Gare de Modane](/wiki/Gare_de_Modane "Gare de Modane"), receiving trains from [Turin](/wiki/Turin "Turin") in [Italy](/wiki/Italy "Italy"), through the [Fréjus Rail Tunnel](/wiki/Fr%C3%A9jus_Rail_Tunnel "Fréjus Rail Tunnel"), and with trains to [Chambéry](/wiki/Chamb%C3%A9ry "Chambéry"), as well as the [TGV](/wiki/TGV "TGV") on the [Milan](/wiki/Milan "Milan") (Italy) to [Paris](/wiki/Paris "Paris") route. The whole of the French part of the line is owned by the [Réseau Ferré de France](/wiki/R%C3%A9seau_Ferr%C3%A9_de_France "Réseau Ferré de France") (RFF), the upstream network is managed by [Rete Ferroviaria Italiana](/wiki/Rete_Ferroviaria_Italiana "Rete Ferroviaria Italiana"), society of FS (*[Ferrovie dello Stato](/wiki/Ferrovie_dello_Stato "Ferrovie dello Stato")*), the lower part and the station being under the management of the [SNCF](/wiki/SNCF "SNCF"). There is therefore an electrical disconnection and signaling changes upstream (catenary supports also bear inscriptions in Italian).

The railway line serving Modane is the [Maurienne line](/wiki/Culoz%E2%80%93Modane_railway "Culoz–Modane railway"), or its official name the *ligne de Culoz à Modane (frontière)* [line of Culoz to Modane (border)]. It marks the territory of the commune of Modane looping 180° between its exit from the station and its entry into the Fréjus tunnel.

## Toponymy

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Modane&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Toponymy")]

The modern spelling *Modane* is not attested before circa. 1700. Previously, there were *Amoudane*, *Amaudane*, *Amaldanus*. The initial vowel has therefore fallen from use (taken for the preposition *à* [to]). The word could come from a proper name, that of the keeper of a *manso*, Amaudanes, mentioned in a cartulary of the 12th century.

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Modane&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: History")]

In 1871, following the opening of the [Mont-Cenis rail tunnel](/wiki/Fr%C3%A9jus_Rail_Tunnel "Fréjus Rail Tunnel") Modane quickly became a border city. The Italian journey began between Italian immigration and transit trade and the population increased significantly. However, the increasingly strained relations between France and Italy lead to the construction in 1885 of the [Fort du Replaton](/wiki/Fort_du_Replaton "Fort du Replaton"), an interdiction fort to monitor the entrance of the tunnel.

During World War I, a train carrying some 1,000 French troops from the Italian front derailed while descending a steep hill in Modane; at least half of the soldiers were killed in France's greatest rail disaster.[[6]](#cite_note-6)

During [World War II](/wiki/World_War_II "World War II"), the city was bombed on 13 or 17 September 1943 by [Allied](/wiki/Allies_of_World_War_II "Allies of World War II") aircraft. The objective of the bombing was the station, an important centre of transit between France and Italy. The bombing caused 60 victims and 100 houses were destroyed. A second bombing took place on the night of 10–11 November 1943, killing five civilians.[[7]](#cite_note-7)

In 1969, the city turned to tourism. A [chairlift](/wiki/Chairlift "Chairlift") departing from [Fourneaux](/wiki/Fourneaux,_Savoie "Fourneaux, Savoie") and joining the hamlet of *Charmaix* on the commune of Modane, at 1,550 metres (5,090 ft) altitude, was indeed created, like the *d'Arrondaz* snow stadium, allowing skiing at 1,550 to 2,500 metres (5,090 to 8,200 ft) altitude.

In 1983, the promoter of Tignes, Pierre Schnebelen, created a new station from *d'Arrondaz*. It built the estate on the hamlet, two cable cars of 1,550 to 2,737 metres (5,085 to 8,980 ft), joining the top of *Punta Bagna*. A new international ski resort was created: [Valfréjus](/wiki/Valfr%C3%A9jus "Valfréjus") (station attached to the municipality of Modane). Between 1983 and 2008, the station evolved to have 7,000 tourist beds.

* The French society of the [Fréjus Road Tunnel](/wiki/Fr%C3%A9jus_Road_Tunnel "Fréjus Road Tunnel") (SFTRF), whose head office is located on the platform of the tunnel in Modane, in addition to the road tunnel manages all of the Maurienne autoroute (connecting [Aiton](/wiki/Aiton,_Savoie "Aiton, Savoie") at the platform of the tunnel of Modane).
* The [Modane Underground Laboratory](/wiki/Modane_Underground_Laboratory "Modane Underground Laboratory") (LSM), centre of [basic research](/wiki/Basic_research "Basic research") of the [IN2P3](/wiki/Institut_national_de_physique_nucl%C3%A9aire_et_de_physique_des_particules "Institut national de physique nucléaire et de physique des particules") (CNRS) and the [CEA](/wiki/Commissariat_%C3%A0_l%27%C3%A9nergie_atomique_et_aux_%C3%A9nergies_alternatives "Commissariat à l'énergie atomique et aux énergies alternatives"), located in the middle of the [Fréjus Road Tunnel](/wiki/Fr%C3%A9jus_Road_Tunnel "Fréjus Road Tunnel")

The company saw its fleet of vehicles very strongly affected by the mudslide at Saint-Antoine in 1987. It had 16 trucks and during the mudslide, it lost thirteen. The company now has five branches: Modane, [Saint-Rémy-de-Maurienne](/wiki/Saint-R%C3%A9my-de-Maurienne "Saint-Rémy-de-Maurienne"), [Montmélian](/wiki/Montm%C3%A9lian "Montmélian") (Chambéry), [Corbas](/wiki/Corbas "Corbas") (Lyon), and [Puiseaux](/wiki/Puiseaux "Puiseaux") (Montargis). The transport capacity is 233 vehicles, recent semi-trailers and trailer trucks, operated as a family business accompanied by 320 employees.
* The Albaron national training centre of the [Red Cross](/wiki/International_Red_Cross_and_Red_Crescent_Movement "International Red Cross and Red Crescent Movement")[[12]](#cite_note-12)
* [SNCF border station of Modane](/wiki/Gare_de_Modane "Gare de Modane")
* [Wind tunnel](/wiki/Wind_tunnel "Wind tunnel") of Modane, [ONERA](/wiki/National_Board_of_Study_and_Aerospace_Research "National Board of Study and Aerospace Research"), the largest in the world. This wind tunnel where many [civil](/wiki/Civil_aviation "Civil aviation") and [military aircraft](/wiki/Military_aircraft "Military aircraft") have been tested mainly employs technicians and a few researchers, actually located administratively on the commune of [Avrieux](/wiki/Avrieux "Avrieux") (adjacent to Modane). It will be closed *"if nothing is done to secure its soil"*, while a renewed interest for wind tunnel testing is emerging in the world, there are yet imperfect digital models.[[13]](#cite_note-13)
* From 1885 to 1993, the [paper mill](/wiki/Paper_mill "Paper mill") of the [Groupe Matussière & Forest](/w/index.php?title=Groupe_Matussi%C3%A8re_%26_Forest&action=edit&redlink=1 "Groupe Matussière & Forest (page does not exist)")[[14]](#cite_note-14) harnessing the power of the [Arc](/wiki/Arc_(Savoie) "Arc (Savoie)") at the entrance of [Fourneaux](/wiki/Fourneaux,_Savoie "Fourneaux, Savoie"), provides work for many people of Modane. The Grenoble papermakers of the Matussiere family gave their name to the current namesake shopping area.

### Tourism

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Modane&action=edit&section=22 "Edit section: Tourism")]

In 2014, the capacity of the commune, estimated by the organisation of [Savoie Mont Blanc](/w/index.php?title=Tourism_in_Pays_de_Savoie&action=edit&redlink=1 "Tourism in Pays de Savoie (page does not exist)") [[fr](https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tourisme_en_pays_de_Savoie "fr:Tourisme en pays de Savoie")], is 7,849 tourist beds in 870 establishments.[[note 1]](#cite_note-16) [Valfréjus](/wiki/Valfr%C3%A9jus "Valfréjus") ski station concentrates most of these beds and structures (over 70%).[[15]](#cite_note-SMB_2014-15) The accommodation of the commune is as follows: 139 [furnished](/wiki/Vacation_rental "Vacation rental"); 8 [apartment hotels](/wiki/Apartment_hotel "Apartment hotel"); 5 [hotels](/wiki/Hotel "Hotel"); an outdoors facility; one [holiday village](/wiki/Holiday_village "Holiday village") and one [gîte](/wiki/G%C3%AEte "Gîte") or overnight gîte.[[15]](#cite_note-SMB_2014-15)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/3/32/Chapelle_Saint-Jacques_%28Modane%29.JPG/250px-Chapelle_Saint-Jacques_%28Modane%29.JPG)](/wiki/File:Chapelle_Saint-Jacques_(Modane).JPG)

The chapel of Saint-Jacques

* The fountain in front of the town hall (*Place de la Mairie*) is listed as an [historical monument](/wiki/Monument_historique "Monument historique"). It dates from 1572.
* The leaning house: The leaning house is located on the road of Modane to [Valfréjus](/wiki/Valfr%C3%A9jus "Valfréjus"). Formerly known as *la poudrière* [the powder magazine], it was used to shelter a stock of powder intended to destroy the tunnel in the event of war. The house was made of dry stone. In 1939, it was overcome by the construction of a concrete blockhouse. In 1944 the Germans dynamited the railway tunnel and the dry stone house collapsed. The blockhouse flew 30 metres (98 ft) and on falling, sank into the ground while remaining intact. When one enters one easily loses balance, because it is tilted at two angles. It is the most visited monument of the Savoie (free visit).[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*] The leaning house is part of the Association of the Saint-Gobain Fort Museum. It belongs to the army, SNCF and the commune of Modane.
* The [Fort du Replaton](/wiki/Fort_du_Replaton "Fort du Replaton") was built to defend the entrance of the railway tunnel at the end of the 19th century. This [Séré de Rivières](/wiki/S%C3%A9r%C3%A9_de_Rivi%C3%A8res_system "Séré de Rivières system")-type fort cannot be visited. The walk departing from Loutraz (30 min) is worthwhile as one can see the illuminations of the fort at night.
* The monumental entrance of the [Fréjus Rail Tunnel](/wiki/Fr%C3%A9jus_Rail_Tunnel "Fréjus Rail Tunnel") was built during the first breakthrough of the Alps between 1857 and 1871. This former entrance of the Fréjus rail tunnel is situated on the road linking Modane to Valfréjus.
* The Saint-Jacques Chapel: A unique baroque building of the town, this small Romanesque chapel, in its construction, has been completely restored. Visits are on request from the association for the restoration of the chapel.
* The parish church: A testimony of reconstruction, it is due to the architect and also author of the best-known church of the [Plateau d'Assy](/wiki/Plateau_d%27Assy "Plateau d'Assy"). Only the bell tower of this building pre-dates [World War II](/wiki/World_War_II "World War II"), the rest having been destroyed during bombings.
* The [Fort of Saint-Gobain](/wiki/Ouvrage_Saint-Gobain "Ouvrage Saint-Gobain"): A structure of the [Maginot Line](/wiki/Maginot_Line "Maginot Line") built in 1933 to protect the national road and railway.[[16]](#cite_note-17)
* Modane internment camp: 539th group of foreign workers in 1940.[[17]](#cite_note-18)

### Museums

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Modane&action=edit&section=27 "Edit section: Museums")]

* The Museobar:[[18]](#cite_note-19) Through four reconstructions of cafes from different eras ranging from 1880 to 1935, the Museobar traces and brings to life the history of Modane and Fourneaux. [Trompe-l'œil](/wiki/Trompe-l%27%C5%93il "Trompe-l'œil"), music of [pianolas](/wiki/Player_piano "Player piano"), testimonies and especially images are illustrations of this extraordinary story.
* The [Rizerie des Alpes](/w/index.php?title=Rizerie_des_Alpes&action=edit&redlink=1 "Rizerie des Alpes (page does not exist)"), a rice mill, an historical monument and labelled as [Heritage of the 20th century](/w/index.php?title=Heritage_of_the_20th_century&action=edit&redlink=1 "Heritage of the 20th century (page does not exist)") [[fr](https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/Label_%22_Patrimoine_du_XXe_si%C3%A8cle_%22 "fr:Label \" Patrimoine du XXe siècle \"")]: It was the function of this "Greek temple" in the middle of the city on the edge of the [Arc](/wiki/Arc_(Savoie) "Arc (Savoie)"). To view in passing, this building is included in the supplementary inventory of historical monuments. Now converted into an exhibition centre for the future [Lyon-Turin](/wiki/Turin%E2%80%93Lyon_high-speed_railway "Turin–Lyon high-speed railway") link.
* Carré Sciences: An interactive space of 120 square metres (1,300 sq ft) dedicated to scientific culture,[[19]](#cite_note-20) with the emanation to the general public of the [Modane Underground Laboratory](/wiki/Modane_Underground_Laboratory "Modane Underground Laboratory") of the [CNRS](/wiki/Centre_national_de_la_recherche_scientifique "Centre national de la recherche scientifique").
